steps.assistant_text: Shift-all-table-entries-by-delta loop (0x14-stride entries at a0->0x20, +delta to entry->0xC, then a0->0xC += delta). Was a permuter-resistant a/v-renumber NM wrap at ~28 reg diffs. Three levers cracked it: (1) declare a third parameter and assign a2 = a1 -- IDO homes the copy in $a2 matching the target's or a2,a1 (function is 2-arg at call sites, K&R-compatible); (2) reuse a1 as the byte-offset induction variable with i declared first (wins $v0); (3) write the update as bump-then-RMW (v1 = (int*)((char*)v1+0xC); *v1 = *v1 + a2;) -- the load becomes an expression temp ($t8 pool instead of named-local $a3) and IDO schedules it before the addiu with the 0xC offset folded. Permuter found the t8-pool hint (score 5 with wrong offset); hand-derived the correct-semantics form.

metadata.verification: Byte-exact 21/21 words vs target asm in clean default build; report.json fuzzy=100.0; NM build also clean.
